pages.raw_text:
From San Francisco comes the
information that President Hill has
| parties of surveyors at work In
northern California. preparatory to
extending the Northern Pacific to


the Golden Gate.


Nothing has thus far been heard
of George Bowlus, who left Wash
| tucna ten days ago. Dementia fol


lowing heavy use of liquor is the
theory of his friends. He sacri
| feed a valuable farm to his appe.
| tite.


Mrs. Lottie McDermott, a resi-.
dent of the vicinity of Forest, was
instantly killed by a tree her hus
band was Telling. The stump of a
limb struck her head.
Freewater, Ore, is the scene of
the latest local. option liquor war.
James McAlrath, to whom a license
has been granted, is under arrest
for selling liquor contrary to the
local option law.


An Okanogan county socialist
has cut down 20 acres of bearing
fruit orchard in order to dodge as
sessment. He commenced cutting
early in the spring and the assessor
found only denuded land.


A fire patrol employed by the
Weyerhaeuser and Lewiston Ma
tional bank interests has succeeded
in saving a vast section of white
pine timber In the mountains above
Orofino.


A Tekoa hardware merchant, J..
e. Reilly, is appealing from de-
cision requiring him to pay poll
tax. Mr.. Reilly claims that 1t is
class legislation and will test the
law of 1905.


Ida Smith, half breed daughter of
Pioneer Abner Smith Of the Yakima
Indian reservation, has married
Frank Iyall, full blood. The bride
is rich in her own name.


Albert w, Howard, one OF the
Lincoln bodyguard at the time of
the assassination, is dead at North
Yakima, aged "a years. He was a
native of New York.


Democrats of Kootenai county,
Idaho. are at Rathdrum today In
convention. Chief interest centers
in the legislative candidates be.
cause OF the Dubois candidacy for
the federal senate.


North Yakima Is in a state OF ex-,
citement over the influx of those
who would file on land recently re.
stored to entry along the Columbia
river. Many slept on boxes while
waiting in line at the land office
for the opening this morning.
